title: Events
subtitle: Workshops and training sessions from the CUNY AI Lab.
series_description: A three-part series on building digital projects with AI, from command-line basics to independent prototyping.
prerequisites: Because the series is scaffolded, participants who are new to the command line and/or Git should plan to attend both the March 3 and March 10 workshops.
Each session is limited to 20 students and will be held in the New Media Lab (Room 7388.01).
Participants are encouraged to bring a laptop so they can set up a workflow on their own machine. Workstations in the New Media Lab are available upon request.

workshops.title: Foundations: AI, Command Line, and Version Control
workshops.description: This workshop covers the fundamentals of using AI tools to develop a digital project, including core AI concepts, setting up your environment, running commands in Terminal or PowerShell, and using Git to track project changes. We'll walk through a basic workflow and use AI to troubleshoot errors as we go.

workshops.title: Vibe-Coding Prototypes
workshops.description: This workshop focuses on using AI via the command line to build a small prototype. We'll model prompting, project design, and troubleshooting. After building the same starter project, participants will choose one feature to customize.
workshops.prerequisite: Prerequisite: Participation in the Foundations workshop, or prior familiarity with the command line and Git/GitHub.

workshops.title: BYOP (Bring Your Own Project) Build Clinic
workshops.description: This session offers structured time to build your own digital scholarly project, with facilitators available to support you. Ahead of the workshop, we'll collect a short description of what you're building and group participants by project type. During the session, each group will work with a facilitator to clarify goals, break work into small steps, and troubleshoot as issues come up.

coworking_description: Drop-in sessions for structured working time on your AI-assisted projects. Bring what you're building and work alongside others — facilitators are available for troubleshooting and feedback.

registration.heading: Register for the Workshop Series
registration.description: Space is limited to 20 participants per session. Sign up early to secure your spot.
